subcategories.name: Legal and regulatory requirements understood
subcategories.description: Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI are understood, managed, and documented across the AI lifecycle.

subcategories.name: Trustworthy AI characteristics integrated
subcategories.description: The characteristics of trustworthy AI are integrated into organizational policies, processes, procedures, and practices.

subcategories.name: Risk processes calibrated to organizational impact
subcategories.description: Processes, procedures, and practices are in place to determine the needed level of risk management activities based on AI system impact level.

evidence.description: 2 finding(s) tagged GOVERN 1.3 from 03-rag-security-auditor
1 canonical rule template(s) tagged GOVERN 1.3 — deployable correlation content for the subcategory
2 finding(s) tagged GOVERN 1.3 from 05-llm-output-compliance-monitor

subcategories.name: Risk management process documented
subcategories.description: The risk management process and outcomes are established with transparent policies, procedures, and roles and responsibilities.

subcategories.name: Continuous monitoring policy
subcategories.description: Ongoing monitoring and periodic review of the AI risk management process and its outcomes is planned.

evidence.description: Continuous monitoring policy: every push runs the eval and pytest suite; regressions break the build
19 hash-chained audit entries — continuous monitoring evidence

subcategories.name: AI deactivation processes
subcategories.description: Processes are in place for end-of-life decommissioning, rollback, or other deactivation of AI systems.

subcategories.name: AI risk responsibilities documented
subcategories.description: Organizational policies and practices are in place to foster a culture of risk awareness across the AI lifecycle.

subcategories.name: Stakeholder engagement processes
subcategories.description: Organizational policies and practices are in place to collect, consider, prioritize, and integrate feedback from those affected by AI systems.

subcategories.name: Third-party AI risks managed
subcategories.description: Policies and procedures are in place to address risks associated with third-party software and data.

subcategories.name: High-priority risks managed first
subcategories.description: A determination is made as to whether the AI system achieves its intended purposes and stated objectives and whether its development or deployment should proceed.

subcategories.name: Treatment of documented AI risks
subcategories.description: Treatment of documented AI risks is prioritized based on impact, likelihood, and available resources or methods.

subcategories.name: Responses to risks selected
subcategories.description: Responses to the AI risks deemed high priority — as identified by the MAP function — including mitigation, transfer, avoidance, and acceptance are developed, planned for, and documented.

subcategories.name: Resources allocated to manage AI risks
subcategories.description: Resources required to manage AI risks are taken into account, along with viable non-AI alternative systems, approaches, or methods to reduce the magnitude or likelihood of potential impacts.

subcategories.name: Mechanisms to override or disengage
subcategories.description: Procedures are followed to respond to and recover from a previously unknown risk when it is identified.

evidence.description: 3 finding(s) tagged MANAGE 2.3 from 02-ai-red-team-framework
1 canonical rule template(s) tagged MANAGE 2.3 — deployable correlation content for the subcategory

subcategories.name: Mechanisms for users to provide feedback
subcategories.description: Mechanisms are in place and applied, and responsibilities are assigned and understood, to supersede, disengage, or deactivate AI systems that demonstrate performance or outcomes inconsistent with intended use.

subcategories.name: Risk monitored over time
subcategories.description: AI risks and benefits from third-party resources are regularly monitored and risk controls are applied and documented.

evidence.description: 5 threat-intel indicators emitted as Splunk notables — third-party risk monitored over time

subcategories.name: Post-deployment monitoring planned
subcategories.description: Post-deployment AI system monitoring plans are implemented, including mechanisms for capturing and evaluating input from users and other relevant AI actors.

evidence.description: 8 rule templates published as deployable Splunk ES / Sigma / Cortex XSIAM correlation content — post-deployment monitoring plans implemented

subcategories.name: Mechanisms for analyzing measurement
subcategories.description: Measurable activities for continual improvements are integrated into AI system updates and include regular engagement with interested parties, including relevant AI actors.

subcategories.name: Incidents documented
subcategories.description: Incidents and errors are communicated to relevant AI actors including affected communities. Processes for tracking, responding to, and recovering from incidents and errors are followed and documented.

evidence.description: 19 audit entries documenting compliance-monitor decisions

subcategories.name: System purpose and context defined
subcategories.description: Intended purpose, context of use, prospective settings, and specific tasks of the AI system are understood and documented.

subcategories.name: Interdisciplinary AI actors engaged
subcategories.description: Interdisciplinary AI actors, competencies, skills, and capacities are inventoried, and capability gaps are identified.

subcategories.name: System tasks and methods documented
subcategories.description: Specific tasks and methods used to implement the tasks the AI system supports are documented.

subcategories.name: Third-party risks documented
subcategories.description: Approaches for mapping AI technology and legal risks of its components — including third-party AI technologies — are documented.

evidence.description: 2 finding(s) tagged MAP 4.1 from 06-model-supply-chain-scanner
Third-party threat-intel feeds mapped to indicators with explicit STIX provenance — third-party AI risks documented

subcategories.name: Internal risk controls documented
subcategories.description: Internal risk controls for AI components are identified and documented.

subcategories.name: Likelihood and magnitude of impacts
subcategories.description: Likelihood and magnitude of each identified impact (both potentially beneficial and harmful) based on expected use, past uses of similar systems, public incident reports, feedback, and input from AI actors are identified and documented.

subcategories.name: Risk measurement approaches identified
subcategories.description: Approaches and metrics for measurement of identified AI risks are selected for implementation, starting with the most significant risks.

evidence.description: Detection eval methodology with explicit F1 threshold — risk measurement approach selected and implemented

subcategories.name: Privacy risk evaluated
subcategories.description: Privacy risk of the AI system — as identified in the MAP function — is examined and documented.

evidence.description: 3 finding(s) tagged MEASURE 2.10 from 02-ai-red-team-framework
15 finding(s) tagged MEASURE 2.10 from 03-rag-security-auditor
1 canonical rule template(s) tagged MEASURE 2.10 — deployable correlation content for the subcategory
8 finding(s) tagged MEASURE 2.10 from 05-llm-output-compliance-monitor

subcategories.name: Fairness and bias evaluated
subcategories.description: Fairness and bias — as identified in the MAP function — are evaluated and results are documented.

subcategories.name: Environmental impact assessed
subcategories.description: Environmental impact and sustainability of AI model training and management activities — as identified in the MAP function — are assessed and documented.

subcategories.name: Computational and physical safety
subcategories.description: AI system is evaluated regularly for safety risks — as identified in the MAP function. The AI system to be deployed is demonstrated to be safe, its residual negative risk does not exceed risk tolerance, and it can fail safely.

evidence.description: 5 finding(s) tagged MEASURE 2.6 from 02-ai-red-team-framework
1 canonical rule template(s) tagged MEASURE 2.6 — deployable correlation content for the subcategory
4 finding(s) tagged MEASURE 2.6 from 05-llm-output-compliance-monitor

subcategories.name: Security and resilience evaluated
subcategories.description: AI system security and resilience — as identified in the MAP function — are evaluated and documented.

evidence.description: Labeled eval set (30 samples) with CI-enforced F1 ≥ 0.85 regression floor — measurable security evaluation
9 finding(s) tagged MEASURE 2.7 from 02-ai-red-team-framework
2 finding(s) tagged MEASURE 2.7 from 03-rag-security-auditor
4 canonical rule template(s) tagged MEASURE 2.7 — deployable correlation content for the subcategory
2 STIX bundle(s) summarized with citation verification; 0 hallucination(s) detected — LLM output measured against source ground truth

subcategories.name: Risks of inferred sensitive data
subcategories.description: Risks associated with transparency and accountability — as identified in the MAP function — are examined and documented.

subcategories.name: Approaches for tracking risks
subcategories.description: Approaches, personnel, and documentation are in place to regularly identify and track existing, unanticipated, and emergent AI risks based on factors such as intended and actual performance in deployed contexts.

subcategories.name: Measurement processes effective
subcategories.description: Measurement approaches for identifying AI risks are connected to deployment context(s) and informed through consultation with domain experts and other end users.

subcategories.name: Measurement results communicated
subcategories.description: Measurement results regarding AI system trustworthiness in deployment context(s) and across the AI lifecycle are informed by input from domain experts and relevant AI actors to validate whether the system is performing consistently as intended.

gaps.name: Mechanisms for users to provide feedback
Resources allocated to manage AI risks
